title: Air-fryer hash browns
subtitle: Crisp shredded potato patties for a full English
excerpt: Grated raw potato, squeezed completely dry and formed into patties, cooked in the air fryer at 200°C for 15 minutes. The drying step is everything: potato is mostly water, and any moisture left in the patty produces steam that prevents the hash brown from crisping.
sourceNotes: Hash browns are an American invention that became part of the British full English breakfast through American diner culture and fast-food chains in the 1980s. The fast-food chain hash brown in particular popularised the compressed, disc-shaped patty that is now the standard form. Homemade versions use raw grated potato rather than the processed product and are crispier when dried properly.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze shaped uncooked patties separated by baking paper. Cook from frozen at 200°C for 18 to 20 minutes.
recipe.batchNotes: Make a large batch and freeze before cooking.

content.text: Squeeze as much moisture from the grated potato as physically possible. Potato is approximately 80% water by weight. Any moisture left in the patty will produce steam during cooking, preventing the surface from crisping. Wring the grated potato in a clean tea towel until no more liquid comes out, then squeeze again.

items.prepNote: Maris Piper, coarsely grated, moisture squeezed out

content.text: Grate the {{potato}} coarsely. Wrap in a clean tea towel and wring out firmly over the sink until no more liquid comes out. Transfer to a bowl and mix with {{sea-salt-fine}}, {{black-pepper}}, and {{vegetable-oil}}. Divide into 4 equal portions and press each firmly into a compact patty about 1 cm thick. The tighter the patty, the better it holds together. Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 3 minutes. Spray the basket with oil. Place the patties in the basket. Spray the tops with oil. Cook at 200°C for 8 minutes, then flip carefully with a spatula and cook for a further 7 minutes until deep golden and crisp on both sides.

items.symptom: Hash browns soft and steaming rather than crisping
items.cause: Insufficient moisture removed, or oil spray missed
items.fix: The wringing step must be very thorough. If the grated potato still feels damp after the first squeeze, repeat. Spray both sides with oil before cooking and again after flipping

items.cause: Potato too wet to bind, or not pressed firmly enough
items.fix: Press the patty as firmly as possible before cooking. A dry, compact patty will hold together. If the mixture is still wet and will not hold shape, add a teaspoon of plain flour as a binder

items.symptom: Exterior dark before the shredded potato inside is cooked through
items.cause: Patty too thick, or temperature too high
items.fix: Keep patties to 1 cm thick. Thicker patties need a lower temperature: reduce to 180°C and cook for 10 minutes per side

content.text: Air-fryer hash browns are a full English breakfast staple. They cook in the same time as the sausages and bacon, making it practical to run multiple components simultaneously. They also work as a side dish for eggs or as part of a brunch plate.
